config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan hanya demi klaim “kepentingan masyarakat,” karena itu mudah disalahgunakan oleh penguasa atau mayoritas untuk menekan yang lemah. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan tetap menghormati hak dasar setiap orang, seperti kebebasan berpendapat, perlindungan hukum, dan martabat pribadi. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika hak individu dilindungi dengan kuat, bukan diposisikan lebih rendah dari kepentingan kolektif.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the idea that society should automatically come before the individual in every case. From my perspective in China, people often value collective stability and social order, and I understand why that matters, especially when a society is large and unevenly developed. But when “the interests of society” become a blank check, they can easily override fairness, silence dissent, and excuse harm to ordinary people. I think the better principle is balance: society has legitimate needs, but individual rights must still set firm limits on what the state or majority can do.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, padahal yang paling rentan justru individu atau kelompok kecil yang tidak punya kuasa. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar, karena tanpa perlindungan itu, stabilitas sosial mudah berubah jadi alat penindasan. Bagi saya, urutannya bukan masyarakat versus individu, melainkan masyarakat yang sehat hanya bisa dibangun kalau hak individu dijaga dulu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should have automatic priority over the individual, because that logic can be used to excuse too much abuse. From a Chinese perspective, I understand why people emphasize stability, coordination, and the common good, since disorder and conflict can hurt ordinary people very quickly. But I also think the strongest systems are the ones that protect individual rights as real limits, not just as promises that can be suspended whenever authorities say it is for the public interest. So for me, society matters, but it should never become a reason to erase fairness, due process, or basic personal dignity.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu karena di Indonesia, dalih “demi masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan yang lemah dan membungkam yang berbeda pendapat. Stabilitas dan kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi kalau hak individu bisa dikesampingkan begitu saja, yang terjadi justru penyalahgunaan kuasa, bukan kebaikan bersama. Saya juga setuju bahwa masyarakat butuh koordinasi dan ketertiban, tetapi batasnya harus jelas: hak dasar, due process, dan martabat pribadi tidak boleh dikorbankan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang selalu mengalahkan individu, melainkan yang melindungi individu agar kepentingan bersama tidak berubah jadi alat penindasan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From my perspective in China, I understand why people value stability and collective goals, because social disorder can hurt millions of ordinary people very fast, but I have also seen how “for the public good” can become a convenient excuse to silence criticism or ignore unfair treatment. If individual rights are treated as secondary, then due process, dignity, and basic fairness become fragile whenever authorities claim a higher social need. To me, a better society is not one that can override the individual whenever it wants, but one that protects people strongly enough that collective goals are pursued without abuse.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
